fracture.base_explanation: A fracture means there is a break or crack in a bone. The seriousness depends on the bone involved and the extent of the break.
fracture.follow_up_note: Doctors assess the type and location of the fracture to decide on appropriate management.

compression fracture.base_explanation: A compression fracture occurs when a bone, commonly in the spine, collapses or becomes compressed.
compression fracture.follow_up_note: Further evaluation helps determine stability and treatment options.

disc bulge.base_explanation: A disc bulge means the cushioning disc between spinal bones slightly extends beyond its usual boundary. This is a common finding and often does not cause symptoms.
disc bulge.follow_up_note: Symptoms and physical examination help determine whether this finding is clinically important.

disc herniation.base_explanation: A disc herniation occurs when part of a spinal disc protrudes further than normal and may come into contact with nearby nerves.
disc herniation.follow_up_note: Clinical symptoms help determine the significance of this finding.

degenerative disc disease.base_explanation: Degenerative disc disease refers to age-related changes in spinal discs that occur gradually over time.
degenerative disc disease.follow_up_note: These changes are common and may or may not be associated with pain.

spondylosis.base_explanation: Spondylosis describes wear-and-tear changes in the spine, including joints and discs, commonly seen with aging.
spondylosis.follow_up_note: Doctors interpret this finding along with symptoms and physical examination.

osteoarthritis.base_explanation: Osteoarthritis refers to gradual wear of joint cartilage over time, which is common as people age.
osteoarthritis.follow_up_note: The degree of symptoms varies and depends on joint involvement.

joint space narrowing.base_explanation: Joint space narrowing means the space between joint surfaces is reduced, often due to cartilage wear.
joint space narrowing.follow_up_note: This finding is commonly evaluated together with symptoms and movement limitations.

osteopenia.base_explanation: Osteopenia indicates lower-than-average bone density, but not to the extent of osteoporosis.
osteopenia.follow_up_note: Bone density measurements help guide further assessment if needed.

osteoporosis.base_explanation: Osteoporosis refers to reduced bone strength, which can increase the risk of fractures.
osteoporosis.follow_up_note: Doctors may recommend further evaluation or tests to assess bone health.
